generated_baseline.policy: Use generated reader source and reader overlays as the reconciliation baseline before any curated reader chapter is treated as release input.

overlay_source.policy: Prefer semantic overlays for section-local reader-only edits; graduate to curated manuscript files only when the edit is too broad for overlays.

graduation_criteria: Generated reader source plus overlays are reviewed and found insufficient for final human-reader prose.
A curated chapter file maps to exactly one manifest chapter ID.
Every curated chapter records its generated-reader baseline, live-source commit or tag, and reconciliation status.
Meaning-changing caveats, support-state limits, implementation horizons, and non-claim boundaries are preserved.
A reconciliation report exists before any reader artifact is rendered from curated source.
The release record names curated reader source only after review actually happens.

companion_note_routing.policy: Track chapter-level companion-note decisions for reader, e-reader, and audio review while preserving meaning-critical caveats in the reader spine.

chapter_review_matrix.policy: Track chapter-by-chapter human-reader review status, overlay disposition, companion-note candidates, curated-manuscript candidates, and release blockers while keeping manifest chapter identity synced to book_structure.json.

format_review_matrix.policy: Track reader format artifact review separately from chapter-text review, and keep every format blocked until artifact review and an edition release record actually exist.

curation_contract.policy: If generated reader source plus overlays stop being enough, curated chapter files may become a parallel derivative prose source only under this contract; they remain subordinate to live-book claims, support states, source boundaries, proof/test status, implementation horizons, and release records.

non_claims: This manifest does not create a reviewed reader release.
